MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. BARRY W. PONTICELLO OF ENGLAND PONTICELLO & ST. CLAIR, TEL. NO. (619) 255-6450 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL D. PADILLA OF O'MARA AND PADILLA, TEL. NO. (858) 481-5454 APPEARING FOR THE INTERVENOR/VERDON;
ATTY. ELIZABETH D. RHODES AND STEPHAN E. KYLE OF KENNEY & MARKOWITZ, LLP, TEL. NO. 397-3100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT U.S. AIRWAYS.

RULING- A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT U.S. AIRWAYS, INC.'S (ERRONEOUSLY AND SERVED HEREIN AS AMERICA WEST AIRLINES) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF SEABRIGHT INSURANCE COMPANY IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO HOOKER AND MADDEN. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT DEFENDANT CONTRIBUTED TO THE CONDITION THAT CAUSED PLAINTIFF'S ACCIDENT BY DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE CONDUCT EVEN ASSUMING DEFENDANT RETAINED SOME CONTROL OVER SAFETY CONDITIONS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT DIRECTED THAT A GUARD OR BETTER LIGHTING NOT BE INSTALLED OR PREVENTED INSTALLATION OR EVEN EVER CONSIDERED THOSE ISSUES. THOSE CONDITIONS WERE OPEN AND OBVIOUS AND PLAINTIFF AND INTERVENOR HAD DISCUSSED THOSE VERY SAFETY CONCERNS BEFORE THE ACCIDENT. PLAINTIFF HAS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT DIRECTED HOW PLAINTIFF AND INTEVENOR WERE TO PERFORM THE TASKS GIVEN THE CONDITIONS OR THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT HAVE TAKEN ITS OWN SAFETY PRECAUTIONS. GIVEN THE LACK OF EVIDENCE OF ANY CONTRIBUTING AFFIRMATIVE CONDUCT BY DEFENDANT, THE SAFETY REGULATIONS DO NOT CREATE A BASIS FOR LIABILITY. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =(301/PJB/LB)
